_note: Example 1: Engineering achievement with strong metrics
action_detail: CI/CD pipeline build time by switching to parallelized GitHub Actions jobs
context: Our monorepo had 200+ services and builds were taking 18+ minutes, blocking 15 engineers daily
stakes: Slow deploys were causing teams to batch changes, increasing blast radius of releases
you_specifically: I independently identified the bottleneck, proposed the parallel job architecture, and implemented it in a 2-week sprint
result_text: Build time dropped from 18 minutes to 11 minutes; team increased deploy frequency from 3x/week to daily
challenge: Legacy Makefile system had no documentation and parallelizing it required rewriting job dependencies
your_decision: Chose parallel job architecture over layer caching to avoid stale artifact risks
what_you_learned: Profiling the build DAG before optimizing saved 3x the effort vs. guessing

_note: Example 2: Product management with business impact
action_detail: a self-serve onboarding flow replacing sales-assisted setup for SMB customers
context: SMB customers were waiting 5–7 days for sales to help them set up, causing 30% trial-to-paid drop
stakes: Fixing conversion would add ~$2M ARR based on existing trial volume
you_specifically: I wrote the spec, ran 8 user interviews, worked with a 3-person eng team, and drove the launch end-to-end
result_text: Trial-to-paid conversion improved from 12% to 19% within 60 days of launch; sales reps freed up 40% of their time
challenge: Engineering team was skeptical — they'd seen two failed attempts at self-serve before
your_decision: Scoped MVP to just 3 setup steps instead of full feature parity to ship in 6 weeks
what_you_learned: Ruthless scoping based on data (80% of users only needed 3 steps) was the unlock

_note: Example 3: Minimal viable atom (for when details are sparse)
action_detail: automated reconciliation tool for monthly financial close process
result_text: Reduced monthly close time from 3 days to 4 hours

action_detail: data engineering team from 3 to 9 engineers over 18 months while maintaining 0 attrition
context: Company was scaling rapidly from seed to Series B with 3x data volume growth
you_specifically: I personally ran all hiring loops, created the leveling rubric, and set up bi-weekly 1:1 cadence and quarterly growth plans
result_text: Team delivered data platform serving 200+ internal users with 99.9% uptime; 3 engineers promoted in 18 months
challenge: Hiring market was extremely competitive; had to build strong employer brand with no brand recognition
